title: `specificFiles` can still re-index Markdown without `includeGlobs`
why: Removing `**/*.md` from `defaultIncludeGlobs` only gates the recursive walk. Selective reindex uses `specificFiles`, which bypasses `config.includeGlobs`, so an already-tracked stale `.md` file can still be parsed and persisted as `language='doc'` without a caller passing `includeGlobs: ['**/*.md']`.
evidence: `ensure-ready.ts:733-737` calls `indexWithTimeout(..., { specificFiles: state.staleFiles })`; `structural-indexer.ts:1472-1508` collects those paths with scope and size checks but no include-glob check; `structural-indexer.ts:2105-2112` uses that bypass branch; `indexer-types.ts:124-125` maps `.md` to `doc`, and `indexer-types.ts:186` keeps `doc` enabled.
recommendation: Apply the effective include-glob policy to `specificFiles` too, or explicitly reject `.md` there unless the resolved scan policy includes a Markdown glob.

title: Opted-in folder matrix test misses real `agents` and `commands` paths
why: The extension-matrix test claims to cover opted-in `.opencode` folders, but uses singular `.opencode/agent` and `.opencode/command` paths. The actual scope policy gates plural `.opencode/agents/**` and `.opencode/commands/**`, so this test does not lock structured config retention for the real agent/command opt-in folders.
evidence: `code-graph-indexer.vitest.ts:620-626` builds paths from `['agent', 'command', 'specs', 'plugins']`; `index-scope-policy.ts:16-17` defines the excluded/opted folders as `**/.opencode/agents/**` and `**/.opencode/commands/**`; `shared/index-scope.ts:71-72` checks `agents` and `commands` segments.
recommendation: Change the matrix entries to `agents` and `commands`, and keep the Markdown exclusion assertion under those real opted-in paths.

title: Inherited object keys bypass unknown-category filtering
why: `resolveMaintainerModeCategories` uses `c in MAINTAINER_CATEGORY_ENV` against a plain object, so inherited names such as `constructor` and `__proto__` are accepted even though they are not maintainer categories. The call site then writes `process.env[MAINTAINER_CATEGORY_ENV[cat]] = 'true'`, which can set env keys beyond the five known `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_*` keys.
evidence: Lines 68-74 define a plain object map; line 83 filters with `c in MAINTAINER_CATEGORY_ENV`; lines 103-104 force-assign using the looked-up value. Runtime check: `resolveMaintainerModeCategories('constructor,__proto__')` returned `constructor,__proto__`, and applying the call-site assignment set `process.env['function Object() { [native code] }']='true'` and `process.env['[object Object]']='true'`. The existing test at lines 22-24 only covers `bogus`, not inherited property names.
recommendation: Filter with an own-property check, e.g. `Object.hasOwn(MAINTAINER_CATEGORY_ENV, c)`, or define the map with a null prototype and still prefer an explicit own-key check. Add a regression test for `constructor,__proto__` resolving to `[]`.

title: Shutdown-path comment still describes re-election as default-off
why: The runtime behavior and unit test now make daemon re-election default-on, but this shutdown-path comment still says default off. That can mislead future reviewers/operators reading the release-vs-kill branch, although the code itself gates correctly through daemonReelectionEnabled().
evidence: .opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:1449 says `Re-election release path (default off)`, while lines 205-206 return true unless SPECKIT_DAEMON_REELECTION is exactly `0` or `off`, and line 1453 uses that helper to enter the release branch. The current unit test asserts default-on at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/launcher-daemon-reelection.vitest.ts:13-20.
recommendation: Update the parenthetical to default-on or remove it so the comment matches the current lifecycle contract.

title: The MK hook-disable rename is not honored end-to-end
why: The four runtime configs are aligned on MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED, but the skill-advisor launcher/daemon paths still forward/read only SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED. Dropping the legacy config key makes a config-level disable toggle ineffective outside the OpenCode plugin path.
evidence: .claude/mcp.json:45, .mcp.json:45, opencode.json:56, and .codex/config.toml:109 set only MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED. .opencode/plugins/mk-skill-advisor.js:38 and :82-86 honor MK plus legacy names, but .opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs:85-129 allowlists SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED and not MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED; .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/handlers/advisor-recommend.ts:414 reads process.env.SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED only.
recommendation: Either keep SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED in the runtime configs until all launcher/daemon/hook consumers accept MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED, or add the MK alias to the launcher allowlist and all disable checks before removing the legacy key.
